meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve can't deploy apps (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Solve Deployment Issues with Antigravity
content.seo_description: Master deployment with the Antigravity project template. A professional guide for freelancers to ship faster using Claude Code and streamlined workflows.
content.answer_block: Deployment friction kills momentum. The Antigravity template standardizes your CI/CD pipeline, abstracting environment configuration into a single, executable manifest. By integrating this with Claude Code, you automate infrastructure provisioning, ensuring your client projects deploy reliably on the first push, every single time, without manual server troubleshooting.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the client deadline is six hours away, and your production environment is throwing a cryptic 502 error. You haven’t slept, the codebase is solid, but the infrastructure is a graveyard of broken dependencies and mismatched environment variables. It’s the silent killer of every high-end freelance career: the deployment bottleneck.

But what if deployment wasn't a chore, but an afterthought? The Antigravity template isn't just code; it’s an architectural paradigm shift. By leveraging AI-first scaffolding and automated orchestration, you stop fighting the server and start focusing on the product. It’s time to stop debugging your infrastructure and start shipping your vision.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the base directory structure with pre-configured Docker and cloud-agnostic deployment manifests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the Antigravity template for a Node.js/TypeScript backend, including automated GitHub Actions for deployment to AWS Fargate.

steps.title: Configure Environment Orchestration
steps.description: Inject client-specific secrets using the Antigravity vault protocol to ensure zero-leak deployments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the project environment variables using the Antigravity vault pattern, separating dev, staging, and production secrets.

steps.title: Automate Infrastructure Provisioning
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map your local architecture to the cloud provider's infrastructure-as-code requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Terraform files for the Antigravity template to provision a managed RDS instance and VPC, optimized for low latency.

steps.title: Integrate CI/CD Pipeline Hooks
steps.description: Define the build-and-test sequence to ensure only passing code hits the production branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow inside the Antigravity template that runs linting, unit tests, and build checks before deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit .github/workflows/deploy.yml --add-step 'npm run test && npm run build'

steps.title: Execute Zero-Downtime Deployment
steps.description: Trigger the final deployment sequence, leveraging blue-green switching to prevent any service interruption.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the final Antigravity deployment command to push the container to production with a blue-green strategy.

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file synced with your Antigravity manifest to prevent configuration drift between local and remote environments.
Leverage Claude Code's 'review' mode on your Terraform plans to catch security vulnerabilities before they are provisioned to the cloud.
Implement automated rollback hooks in your Antigravity config; if a health check fails post-deployment, the system reverts automatically.
Use ephemeral staging environments for client previews, destroying them immediately after sign-off to minimize cloud costs.
Tag all infrastructure resources with client-specific identifiers to allow for easy billing reconciliation and resource tracking.
resources: Terraform Best Practices for AI Builders: https://terraform.io/guides
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is successful, document your custom Antigravity configuration as a private repository template to reuse across all future client projects.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting deployment friction dictate your freelance growth. By standardizing your workflow with Antigravity and Claude Code, you gain the freedom to take on more complex projects with total confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
